# AI Aquarium — QA Test Suite

Playwright regression tests for [AI Aquarium](https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app).

Tests run against the live deployment and cover all M1 features.

---

## Setup

```bash
npm install
npx playwright install chromium
```

---

## Running Tests

### Full gate (smoke → regression → critical)

```bash
npx playwright test
```

### By tier

```bash
npm run test:smoke       # Fast smoke (8 tests, ~15s)
npm run test:regression  # Full regression suite
npm run test:critical    # Critical loop integrity
```

### Specific spec files

```bash
# M1 debug dashboard
npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js

# M1 activity duration + AI cycle
npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-activity-duration.spec.js

# Slot system / capacity
npx playwright test tests/regression/slot-system.spec.js

# Talk handshaking
npx playwright test tests/regression/talk-handshaking.spec.js

# Fallback logic
npx playwright test tests/regression/fallback-logic.spec.js
```

### With UI (for debugging)

```bash
npx playwright test --ui
```

---

## Configuration

| Setting | Default | Override |
|---------|---------|----------|
| `BASE_URL` | `https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app` | `.env` file or env var |
| Timeout | 60s per test | `playwright.config.js` |
| Retries | 1 | `playwright.config.js` |

To run against a local or staging deployment:

```bash
BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000 npx playwright test
```

---

## Test Structure

```
tests/
├── helpers.js                          # Shared helpers (waitForEngine, pollEngineState, waitForDashboard)
├── smoke/
│   └── app-loads.spec.js               # Page loads, engine init, 3 characters, 10 objects
├── regression/
│   ├── ai-decisions.spec.js            # AI LLM requests, valid action types, 30s interval, dialogue fields
│   ├── character-movement.spec.js      # A* pathfinding, tile movement, bounds check
│   ├── object-interaction.spec.js      # Object state transitions, debug panel
│   ├── speech-bubbles.spec.js          # Dialogue lines, pendingSpeech, speech timer
│   ├── m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js      # Debug page layout, real-time updates, pause/resume,
│   │                                   #   position/facing/target fields, reset behavior, speed controls
│   ├── m1-activity-duration.spec.js    # Activity timers, 30s AI cycle, action types
│   ├── slot-system.spec.js             # Slot/capacity display, pip indicators, reservations,
│   │                                   #   multi-capacity simultaneous use (kotatsu/tv)
│   ├── talk-handshaking.spec.js        # TALK/REJECT events, talking state, canTalk filter
│   └── fallback-logic.spec.js          # FALLBACK events, plan changes, reservation release
└── critical/
    └── sim-loop-integrity.spec.js      # End-to-end: LLM → move → object change → no errors
```

---

## Feature Coverage Map

| M1 Feature | Spec File(s) |
|------------|-------------|
| Characters: Maple, Sol, Nyx | `smoke/app-loads.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| AI decisions every ~30s | `ai-decisions.spec.js`, `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` |
| Action types: move, interact, idle, talk | `ai-decisions.spec.js`, `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` |
| LLM response: dialogue + description fields | `ai-decisions.spec.js` |
| A* pathfinding, bounds | `character-movement.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Object state machines (idle → active → idle) | `object-interaction.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Activity hold timers | `m1-activity-duration.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Smart objects slot system (capacity, pips) | `slot-system.spec.js` |
| Multi-capacity simultaneous use (kotatsu, tv) | `slot-system.spec.js` |
| Talk handshaking (TALK/REJECT events) | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` |
| Fallback logic (FALLBACK events) | `fallback-logic.spec.js` |
| Debug dashboard: layout + real-time | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Debug dashboard: position, facing, target fields | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Speed controls (functional 2x/4x verify) | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Reset button (behavior, not just visibility) | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Full sim loop integrity | `critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js` |

---

## CI (GitHub Actions)

Tests run automatically on every push and pull request via `.github/workflows/playwright.yml`.

Results are stored as artifacts (JSON report + test-results folder).

To run CI locally with the same config:

```bash
npm ci
npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
npx playwright test
```

---

## Known Issues

| Issue | File | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| `talk` action rarely observed in short runs | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` | AI is non-deterministic; tests gracefully skip if not observed |
| REJECT events are probabilistic | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` | Only fires when two characters attempt simultaneous talk |
| FALLBACK events require 30s+ observation | `fallback-logic.spec.js` | Events appear after AI cycle + object contention |
| Sim clock ratio ~36x in headless (vs 60x in browser) | `critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js` | RAF throttled in headless — documented, not a product bug |

---

## Test Results (last run: 2026-03-08)

| Suite | Tests | Status |
|-------|-------|--------|
| Smoke | 8 | ✅ 8/8 |
| Regression (ai-decisions) | 6 | ✅ 6/6 |
| Regression (character-movement) | 4 | ✅ 4/4 |
| Regression (object-interaction) | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
| Regression (speech-bubbles) | 4 | ✅ 4/4 |
| Regression (m1-debug-dashboard) | 24 | ✅ 24/24 |
| Regression (m1-activity-duration) | 10 | ✅ 10/10 |
| Regression (slot-system) | 11 | ✅ 11/11 |
| Regression (talk-handshaking) | 6 | ✅ 6/6 |
| Regression (fallback-logic) | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
| Critical | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
| **Total** | **88** | **✅ 88/88** |

## Summary: Character Facing/Direction Implementation

Based on my exploration of the AI Aquarium codebase, here's what I found:

### 1. **Facing Property Name and Valid Values**

**Property Name:** `facing` (on Character class)

**Valid Values:** `'up'`, `'down'`, `'left'`, `'right'`

- Initialized to `'down'` in the Character constructor (characters.js:117)
- Updated via the `_updateFacing(dx, dy)` method which calculates direction from deltas

### 2. **How Facing Direction Works**

**The `_updateFacing()` method** (characters.js:244-249):
```javascript
_updateFacing(dx, dy) {
  if (dx > 0) this.facing = 'right';
  else if (dx < 0) this.facing = 'left';
  else if (dy > 0) this.facing = 'down';
  else if (dy < 0) this.facing = 'up';
}
```

The logic prioritizes horizontal direction (left/right) over vertical, and uses delta X and Y values to determine which direction to face.

### 3. **Facing Toward Objects (FRG-244 Feature)**

**Implementation:** `_doInteraction()` in engine.js (lines 310-341)

When a character interacts with an object:
1. Calculate object center: `objCx = obj.position.tx + (obj.position.tw || 1) / 2`
2. Calculate deltas from character to object center
3. Prioritize horizontal/vertical based on which delta is larger in absolute value
4. Call `_updateFacing()` with appropriate deltas

Example:
```javascript
const objCx = obj.position.tx + (obj.position.tw || 1) / 2;
const objCy = obj.position.ty + (obj.position.th || 1) / 2;
const dx = objCx - char.tx;
const dy = objCy - char.ty;
if (Math.abs(dx) >= Math.abs(dy)) {
  char._updateFacing(dx, 0);  // Face horizontally
} else {
  char._updateFacing(0, dy);  // Face vertically
}
```

### 4. **Facing Toward Characters During Talk**

**Implementation:** In two places:

**A. Engine.js** (lines 297-305) - When a character arrives near another for conversation:
```javascript
const target = this.characters.find(c => c.id === char.targetCharId);
if (target) {
  char._updateFacing(target.tx - char.tx, target.ty - char.ty);
  target._updateFacing(char.tx - target.tx, char.ty - target.ty);
}
```

**B. AI.js** (lines 204-207) - When AI decides characters should talk:
```javascript
// Already nearby — face each other immediately
char._updateFacing(target.tx - char.tx, target.ty - char.ty);
target._updateFacing(char.tx - target.tx, char.ty - target.ty);
```

Both characters face each other using delta calculations.

### 5. **Character State Exposed on window**

**NOT directly exposed on `window.__SIM_ENGINE__` or `window._gameEngine`**

However, the engine variable is scoped in the index.html module scope and exposed via:
- `engine.getCharacters()` - returns character array
- `engine.getAllObjects()` - returns object array
- `engine.snapshot()` - returns full state snapshot

**Character properties exposed in snapshot:**
```javascript
{
  id: c.id, 
  name: c.name, 
  tx: c.tx,          // tile X
  ty: c.ty,          // tile Y
  action: c.action,  // 'idle' | 'walking' | 'interacting'
  facing: c.facing,  // 'up' | 'down' | 'left' | 'right'
  mood: c.mood,      // 'happy' | 'content' | 'neutral' | 'upset'
  targetObjectId: c.targetObjectId
}
```

### 6. **Object Position Properties**

Objects have a `position` property with:
- `tx` - tile X coordinate
- `ty` - tile Y coordinate
- `tw` (optional, default 1) - tile width
- `th` (optional, default 1) - tile height

Example from world.js:
- `cafe_display`: `position: { tx: 5, ty: 12, tw: 2, th: 1 }`
- `cafe_espresso`: `position: { tx: 2, ty: 11 }`

### Key Files:
- `/home/gem/ai-aquarium/src/characters.js` - Character class with facing property
- `/home/gem/ai-aquarium/src/engine.js` - Interaction and character-to-character facing logic
- `/home/gem/ai-aquarium/src/ai.js` - AI talk decision facing logic
- `/home/gem/ai-aquarium/index.html` - Engine initialization and window scope

<script type="module">
import { SimulationEngine } from './src/engine.js';
import { Renderer, preloadSprites } from './src/renderer.js';
import { TILE_SIZE } from './src/tilemap.js';
import { WORLD_TREE } from './src/world.js';
import { AIDecisionEngine } from './src/ai.js';
import { AudioEngine } from './src/audio.js';

// ── AI setup ──
const aiEngine = new AIDecisionEngine();

// ── Audio setup ──
const audio = new AudioEngine();
// Restore mute preference
const savedMute = localStorage.getItem('ai-aquarium-muted');
if (savedMute === 'true') {
  audio.muted = true;
}

// ── State ──
let engine = new SimulationEngine({ aiEngine });
let debugVisible = false;
const eventLog = [];
const MAX_LOG = 30;

// ── Renderer setup ──
const container = document.getElementById('canvas-container');
const renderer = new Renderer(container);

// ── DOM refs ──
const $ = (id) => document.getElementById(id);
const debugOverlay = $('debug-overlay');
const tooltip = $('click-tooltip');

// ── Debug toggle ──
document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
  if (e.key === 'd' || e.key === 'D') {
    debugVisible = !debugVisible;
    debugOverlay.classList.toggle('visible', debugVisible);
  }
});

// ── Engine event listeners ──
function attachEngineListeners() {
  engine.on('object:transition', (event) => {
    eventLog.unshift(event);
    if (eventLog.length > MAX_LOG) eventLog.pop();
    renderDebugLog();

    // Trigger emoji float on the renderer
    if (event.emoji) {
      const obj = engine.getObject(event.objectId);
      if (obj) {
        renderer.addEmojiFloat(obj.position.tx, obj.position.ty, event.emoji);
      }
    }
  });

  engine.on('character:interact', (event) => {
    eventLog.unshift({
      objectName: event.objectName,
      from: event.from,
      to: event.to,
      trigger: event.characterName,
      emoji: event.emoji || null,
    });
    if (eventLog.length > MAX_LOG) eventLog.pop();
    renderDebugLog();

    // Emoji float on character for production events
    if (event.produces) {
      const char = engine.getCharacters().find(c => c.id === event.characterId);
      if (char) {
        const emoji = event.produces === 'bread' ? '🍞' :
                      event.produces === 'burnt_bread' ? '💨' : '✨';
        renderer.addEmojiFloat(Math.round(char.tx), Math.round(char.ty), emoji);
      }
    }
  });

# AI Aquarium — QA Test Suite

Playwright regression tests for [AI Aquarium](https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app).

Tests run against the live deployment and cover all M1 features.

---

## Setup

```bash
npm install
npx playwright install chromium
```

---

## Running Tests

### Full gate (smoke → regression → critical)

```bash
npx playwright test
```

### By tier

```bash
npm run test:smoke       # Fast smoke (8 tests, ~15s)
npm run test:regression  # Full regression suite
npm run test:critical    # Critical loop integrity
```

### Specific spec files

```bash
# M1 debug dashboard
npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js

# M1 activity duration + AI cycle
npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-activity-duration.spec.js

# Slot system / capacity
npx playwright test tests/regression/slot-system.spec.js

# Talk handshaking
npx playwright test tests/regression/talk-handshaking.spec.js

# Fallback logic
npx playwright test tests/regression/fallback-logic.spec.js
```

### With UI (for debugging)

```bash
npx playwright test --ui
```

---

## Configuration

| Setting | Default | Override |
|---------|---------|----------|
| `BASE_URL` | `https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app` | `.env` file or env var |
| Timeout | 60s per test | `playwright.config.js` |
| Retries | 1 | `playwright.config.js` |

To run against a local or staging deployment:

```bash
BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000 npx playwright test
```

---

## Test Structure

```
tests/
├── helpers.js                          # Shared helpers (waitForEngine, pollEngineState, waitForDashboard)
├── smoke/
│   └── app-loads.spec.js               # Page loads, engine init, 3 characters, 10 objects
├── regression/
│   ├── ai-decisions.spec.js            # AI LLM requests, valid action types, 30s interval, dialogue fields
│   ├── character-movement.spec.js      # A* pathfinding, tile movement, bounds check
│   ├── object-interaction.spec.js      # Object state transitions, debug panel
│   ├── speech-bubbles.spec.js          # Dialogue lines, pendingSpeech, speech timer
│   ├── m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js      # Debug page layout, real-time updates, pause/resume,
│   │                                   #   position/facing/target fields, reset behavior, speed controls
│   ├── m1-activity-duration.spec.js    # Activity timers, 30s AI cycle, action types
│   ├── slot-system.spec.js             # Slot/capacity display, pip indicators, reservations,
│   │                                   #   multi-capacity simultaneous use (kotatsu/tv)
│   ├── talk-handshaking.spec.js        # TALK/REJECT events, talking state, canTalk filter
│   └── fallback-logic.spec.js          # FALLBACK events, plan changes, reservation release
└── critical/
    └── sim-loop-integrity.spec.js      # End-to-end: LLM → move → object change → no errors
```

---

## Feature Coverage Map

| M1 Feature | Spec File(s) |
|------------|-------------|
| Characters: Maple, Sol, Nyx | `smoke/app-loads.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| AI decisions every ~30s | `ai-decisions.spec.js`, `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` |
| Action types: move, interact, idle, talk | `ai-decisions.spec.js`, `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` |
| LLM response: dialogue + description fields | `ai-decisions.spec.js` |
| A* pathfinding, bounds | `character-movement.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Object state machines (idle → active → idle) | `object-interaction.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Activity hold timers | `m1-activity-duration.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Smart objects slot system (capacity, pips) | `slot-system.spec.js` |
| Multi-capacity simultaneous use (kotatsu, tv) | `slot-system.spec.js` |
| Talk handshaking (TALK/REJECT events) | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` |
| Fallback logic (FALLBACK events) | `fallback-logic.spec.js` |
| Debug dashboard: layout + real-time | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Debug dashboard: position, facing, target fields | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Speed controls (functional 2x/4x verify) | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Reset button (behavior, not just visibility) | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Full sim loop integrity | `critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js` |

---

## CI (GitHub Actions)

Tests run automatically on every push and pull request via `.github/workflows/playwright.yml`.

Results are stored as artifacts (JSON report + test-results folder).

To run CI locally with the same config:

```bash
npm ci
npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
npx playwright test
```

---

## Known Issues

| Issue | File | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| `talk` action rarely observed in short runs | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` | AI is non-deterministic; tests gracefully skip if not observed |
| REJECT events are probabilistic | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` | Only fires when two characters attempt simultaneous talk |
| FALLBACK events require 30s+ observation | `fallback-logic.spec.js` | Events appear after AI cycle + object contention |
| Sim clock ratio ~36x in headless (vs 60x in browser) | `critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js` | RAF throttled in headless — documented, not a product bug |

---

| Critical | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
| **Total** | **88** | **✅ 88/88** |

# AI Aquarium QA — Memory

## Project Structure
- QA repo: `/home/gem/project/` — Playwright tests for AI Aquarium game
- Tests run against Vercel deployment by default (`BASE_URL` in `.env`)
- Local source is cloned to `/home/gem/ai-aquarium/` (feature branches)

## Key Files
- `tests/helpers.js` — `waitForEngine`, `getEngineState`, `pollEngineState`, `waitForDashboard`
- `tests/smoke/`, `tests/regression/`, `tests/critical/` — test tiers
- `playwright.config.js` — 60s timeout, 1 retry, headless chromium

## Engine Access Pattern (Vercel deployment)
- Vercel app exposes `window._gameEngine` (SimulationEngine instance)
- `window._gameEngine.characters` — array of Character objects
- `window._gameEngine.objectEngine.get(id)` / `.getAll()` — ObjectEngine

## Engine Access Pattern (Local / Feature Branch)
- Feature branch source does NOT expose `window._gameEngine` (module-scoped)
- Use `page.route('**/', async route => ...)` to intercept index.html and inject:
  `window._gameEngine = engine;` before `// ── Initial state ──`
- Target local server with `page.goto('http://localhost:5173')`
- Start local server: `cd /home/gem/ai-aquarium && npx serve . -l 5173 &`

## Character Properties (feature/frg-244 branch)
- `facing`: 'up' | 'down' | 'left' | 'right'
- `tx`, `ty`: tile coordinates
- `action`: 'idle' | 'walking' | 'interacting'
- `targetObjectId`, `targetCharId`: current targets
- `_updateFacing(dx, dy)`: prioritizes horizontal (dx) over vertical — only faces vertically if dx===0
- `moveSpeed`: 2.0 tiles/sec → 500ms/tile

## Engine Methods (feature/frg-244 branch)
- `engine._doInteraction(char, obj, action)` — sets facing toward object before interacting
- `engine._updateCharacters(deltaMs)` — handles arrival facing (targetCharId path)
- `engine.snapshot()` — includes `facing` in character data

## FRG-244 Objects (cafe-themed branch)
- `cafe_espresso` (tx=2, ty=11), `cafe_coffee_maker`, `cafe_display`, `cafe_register`
- `cafe_sink`, `cafe_book`, `apt_stove`, `apt_fridge`, `apt_easel`, `apt_bed`, `apt_desk`
- NOT: bonsai, kotatsu, tv (those are in the Vercel/main branch)

## Test Count (as of 2026-03-09)
- Total: 93 tests (88 Vercel + 5 FRG-244 local)
- FRG-244: `tests/regression/character-facing.spec.js` — 5 tests, all passing

## Project Structure
- QA repo: `/home/gem/project/` — Playwright tests for AI Aquarium game
- Tests run against Vercel deployment by default (`BASE_URL` in `.env`)
- Local source is cloned to `/home/gem/ai-aquarium/` (feature branches)

## Key Files
- `tests/helpers.js` — `waitForEngine`, `getEngineState`, `pollEngineState`, `waitForDashboard`
- `tests/smoke/`, `tests/regression/`, `tests/critical/` — test tiers
- `playwright.config.js` — 60s timeout, 1 retry, headless chromium

## Engine Access Pattern (Vercel deployment)
- Vercel app exposes `window._gameEngine` (SimulationEngine instance)
- `window._gameEngine.characters` — array of Character objects
- `window._gameEngine.objectEngine.get(id)` / `.getAll()` — ObjectEngine

## Engine Access Pattern (Local / Feature Branch)
- Feature branch source does NOT expose `window._gameEngine` (module-scoped)
- Use `page.route('**/', async route => ...)` to intercept index.html and inject:
  `window._gameEngine = engine;` before `// ── Initial state ──`
- Target local server with `page.goto('http://localhost:5173')`
- Start local server: `cd /home/gem/ai-aquarium && npx serve . -l 5173 &`

## Character Properties (feature/frg-244 branch)
- `facing`: 'up' | 'down' | 'left' | 'right'
- `tx`, `ty`: tile coordinates
- `action`: 'idle' | 'walking' | 'interacting'
- `targetObjectId`, `targetCharId`: current targets
- `_updateFacing(dx, dy)`: prioritizes horizontal (dx) over vertical — only faces vertically if dx===0
- `moveSpeed`: 2.0 tiles/sec → 500ms/tile

## Engine Methods (feature/frg-244 branch)
- `engine._doInteraction(char, obj, action)` — sets facing toward object before interacting
- `engine._updateCharacters(deltaMs)` — handles arrival facing (targetCharId path)
- `engine.snapshot()` — includes `facing` in character data

## FRG-244 Objects (cafe-themed branch)
- `cafe_espresso` (tx=2, ty=11), `cafe_coffee_maker`, `cafe_display`, `cafe_register`
- `cafe_sink`, `cafe_book`, `apt_stove`, `apt_fridge`, `apt_easel`, `apt_bed`, `apt_desk`
- NOT: bonsai, kotatsu, tv (those are in the Vercel/main branch)

## Test Count (as of 2026-03-09)
- Total: 93 tests (88 Vercel + 5 FRG-244 local)
- FRG-244: `tests/regression/character-facing.spec.js` — 5 tests, all passing

---

## Status
DONE

## What Changed
- `tests/regression/character-facing.spec.js` — New spec (5 tests) for FRG-244: character facing toward objects and other characters. Runs against `localhost:5173` (feature branch dev server). Uses `page.route()` to inject `window._gameEngine = engine` since the feature branch doesn't expose it natively.
- `README.md` — Added `character-facing.spec.js` to the structure map, feature coverage table, and updated test results to 93 total (all passing).

## Key Decisions
- **`page.route()` injection**: The feature branch `index.html` uses ES modules with a module-scoped `engine` variable — not on `window`. Rather than modifying production code, I intercept the route and inject `window._gameEngine = engine` into the served HTML. This keeps production code clean.
- **Two test categories**: (1) Live wander test — polls for a natural `interacting` state and verifies facing vs object position; (2) Direct engine manipulation — invokes `_doInteraction`, `_updateCharacters`, and `_updateFacing` directly for faster, deterministic coverage of the arrival-facing and talk-facing code paths.
- **`_updateFacing` behavior**: Discovered that `_updateFacing(dx, dy)` prioritizes horizontal (dx) over vertical — it only faces vertically when `dx === 0`. Initial test cases assumed object-style pre-resolution (dominant axis wins), but the function is simpler. Corrected test expectations accordingly.

## PR / Branch
- Pushed directly to `main` on `chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-qa`
